title: torilis leptophylla var. leptophylla
intro: Torilis leptophylla var. leptophylla, also known as hemlock parsley or bee eggs, is a biennial plant in the carrot family (Apiaceae) that is native to Europe and parts of Asia. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory effects, which are thought to be due to the presence of sesquiterpenes and other compounds. The health benefits of Torilis leptophylla var. leptophylla are said to include relief from digestive issues, menstrual cramps, and respiratory problems, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
